content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Orac and Models". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1350/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. The first line of each test case contains two integers n and m. The second line contains n integers a1, a2, ..., an. The third line contains m integers b1, b2, ..., bm.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum number of pairs that can be formed.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Sort both arrays and then try to form pairs from the smallest elements of both arrays.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    n, m = map(int, input().split())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    a.sort()
    b.sort()
    i = j = count = 0
    while i < n and j < m:
        if a[i] <= b[j]:
            i += 1
            j += 1
            count += 1
        else:
            j += 1
    print(count)
